title: Chionanthus thorelii
taxonomy: Chionanthus thorelii is classified in the family Oleaceae, which includes other flowering plants commonly known as olives and ash trees. This species is further grouped into the genus Chionanthus, a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Chionanthus thorelii is a flowering plant that is known for its fragrant flowers and is often used as an ornamental plant. Its classification also includes the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their bilateral symmetry and two-lipped flowers. Chionanthus thorelii is also classified as a monocot, a group of plants that have one cotyledon in their seeds.
uses: Chionanthus thorelii has been traditionally used in Vietnamese medicine, and some sources suggest its leaves may have antimicrobial properties.
